h1: Does EEOICPA Cover Wound Care at Home?
title: Does EEOICPA Cover Home Wound Care? White Card Wound Care in Las Vegas | Alara
metaDescription: Yes — EEOICPA White Card benefits can cover skilled home wound care at no out-of-pocket cost for approved DOE and Nevada Test Site workers. Here's what's covered and how to start.
leadAnswer: Yes. For approved EEOICPA beneficiaries, skilled wound care delivered at home is generally covered under White Card medical benefits at no out-of-pocket cost when ordered by a physician. This includes wound assessment, dressing changes, infection monitoring, and nurse-led treatment of chronic or non-healing wounds.
whatsCovered: Skilled nursing wound assessment and staging
Sterile dressing changes and wound-bed management
Negative-pressure / advanced wound therapy where ordered
Caregiver education on wound care between visits
howItWorks: A physician orders home wound care; Alara verifies White Card eligibility and the accepted/consequential condition, secures authorization, and begins nurse-led visits. Beneficiaries typically pay nothing out of pocket.
alaraRole: Nurse-led skilled wound care across Clark County, with a Director of Nursing reviewing every case and zero tolerance for authorization gaps.

faqs.q: Do I pay anything for White Card wound care at home?
faqs.a: Approved EEOICPA beneficiaries generally pay nothing out of pocket for covered, physician-ordered home wound care.

faqs.q: Does EEOICPA cover wound care for a consequential condition?
faqs.a: If a wound results from a Department of Labor–accepted consequential condition, related wound care may be covered. Alara helps document and verify this.

gateChecklist.truthfulness: pending SME confirm vs DOL source
